assertion_results.text: Response describes the 4-step implicit token flow: popup opens → user consents → access token returned directly to JS callback → no server exchange required
assertion_results.evidence: Numbered list: '1. initTokenClient… 2. popup opens with consent screen 3. user grants → access token directly to JavaScript callback 4. No redirect, no authorization code, no server exchange'

assertion_results.text: Response explicitly states that JavaScript Origins should be set and redirect URIs should NOT be added to GCP Console
assertion_results.evidence: '⛔ Do NOT add Authorized redirect URIs — the token model does not use them. Leave the redirect URIs section completely empty.'

assertion_results.text: Response references window.google.accounts.oauth2.initTokenClient by exact API name
assertion_results.evidence: `window.google.accounts.oauth2.initTokenClient({ client_id, scope, callback })` — exact name with window. prefix

assertion_results.text: Response clarifies the Client ID is intentionally public and safe to embed — Client Secret intentionally absent from this model
assertion_results.evidence: 'The token model uses no Client Secret — it is intentionally absent from this flow'
